notes.anchor: Two letters from you have reached me of the same content
notes.note: Sending duplicate copies of a letter by separate carriers was standard practice to guard against loss on the long, insecure route between Gaul and Rome; Cicero reads it as a mark of Plancus's care.
"Brigands" (latrones) is Cicero's standing term of abuse for Antony and his armed partisans, casting them as outlaws rather than legitimate magistrates.

notes.anchor: I advise you as a son
notes.note: Cicero, some twenty years Plancus's senior and tied to his family, claims a quasi-paternal authority; the triple figure (as a son, as myself, as your closest friend) marks the warmth and urgency of the appeal.
